seen.summary: Henry Schreiner reports using Fable for repository reviews across dozens of OSS projects, finding ~400 bugs and 400+ other issues, with several hundred PRs and near-100% merge rate on decided PRs.
Post-return Fable review of pipx: four review passes, top findings verified, two reproduced locally, includes high/medium severity packaging-tool bugs.
Post-return Fable review of meson-python: no high-severity bugs, but detailed ranked findings with line references across correctness, performance, simplification, and modernization.
Fable review of PyPA packaging found an unreleased release-blocking regression and other verified bugs; author independently reproduced high-severity findings.

Claude Code/Fable-labeled PR fixing four robustness issues in an agent fan-out dispatcher, including dependency-failure propagation, safe worktree auto-repair, idle-poll reaping, and tmux pane cleanup, with targeted tests.
Fable-triaged Excel parity batch for a spreadsheet app: five gated fixes from miner findings, including criteria functions, formula fill, number-format rendering, edit fidelity, and undo handling.
Claude Code/Fable PR fixing VST engine polyphony with fixed-size voice pools and deterministic audio tests proving overlapping drum hits.
Claude Fable 5 PR adding a non-allocating Julia point-evaluation kernel for ContinuousDPs.jl, with 260 new tests and benchmarked 2x-4x speedups / large allocation reductions.
Claude Fable 5 co-authored Julia compiler subtype optimization skipping typevar aliasing checks for closed queries, recovering about half of a package-image load-time regression.

Claude Fable 5 PR from deep Signal K plugin API review, fixing real-server self-context duplication, async startup rejection handling, logging attribution, dashboard write-rate reporting, and status/error routing.
Fable 5-labeled PR making a music-practice app preserve transcribed MIDI velocity and sustain-pedal data retroactively, with parser tests and production-build validation.

Claude Fable 5 co-authored performance rewrite of an i18n hardcoded-string scanner, skipping tree-sitter on clean files and rewriting similarity matching, with byte-identical verification and up to 14x clean-corpus speedups.

Claude Fable 5 co-authored codebase review plus critical security fixes for a site-builder app: domain takeover prevention, server-side session resolution, CORS/CSRF tightening, data leak reduction, and owner-gated builder access.
BRIDGE-bench expansion reports Claude Fable 5 refusing defensive smart-contract exploit analysis across multiple framings, while Opus 4.8 produced measurable results after semantic rescoring.
Claude Fable 5-authored PR adding GGUF array metadata extraction and wiring per-layer attention/feed-forward settings into a llama loader, with parser/load tests and existing C suites reported green.
Claude Fable 5 co-authored Brunch doctor-pass fixes from walkthrough findings: provider-prompt guarding, startup/kick UX improvements, Other-answer schema repair, and expanded tests across 26 files.

Claude Fable 5-authored Memoria TCG expansion and tuning batch with six new Showdown rivals, Bible sub-element mechanics, generated art prompts, simulation win-rate tuning, and 197 reported tests green.

Claude Fable 5-authored UX Auto Reviewer overhaul adding normalized-page adapters, auth, tenant directory separation, declarative rule overlays, AI rule authoring, LLM abstraction, and 447 reported tests green.
Claude Fable 5 co-authored production-deployed multi-tenant memory migration and fail-closed account-resolution hardening, with transaction backup, deduplication, and 1674 tests.
Claude Fable 5 co-authored terminal escape-sequence hardening for a TUI/intel renderer, sanitizing C0/C1 controls after XML entity expansion and before ratatui spans.
Claude Fable 5 co-authored a deterministic parallel ingest parser with scheduling-fuzz parity tests and fixed parse-timeout enforcement after profiling a bash grammar hotspot.
Fable 5 audit decision fixes for a scientific manuscript: corrected an overclaim about ribosome-like error rate and a mutual-information value exceeding its channel ceiling.
PostHog log-transformations stack layer 1: Fable 5 rebased stale API/type work, sliced a four-PR stack, and caught a compiler validation gap where async Hog functions were accepted.
PostHog log-transformations stack layer 2: Fable 5 built a high-volume HogVM log transformation engine with fail-open budgets, aggregation, benchmarks, and live attribute encoding validation.
PostHog log-transformations stack layer 3: Fable 5 wired the engine into logs ingestion behind independent gates, with consumer tests and local end-to-end ClickHouse validation.
PostHog log-transformations stack layer 4: Fable 5 rebased UI work, added templates and inline testing, closed UX parity gaps, and ran focused frontend/API tests.

Claude Code Fable 5-authored guard-hardening batch for a Claude statusline/MCP tool: stdio smoke, OS matrix, hot-path latency budget, pack guard, release QA, SHA-pinned workflows, atomic settings writes, and 627 local tests.

Claude Fable 5 co-authored a LiveKit real-time voice layer for an autonomous AI agency dashboard, adding token endpoints, a worker pipeline with VAD/STT/LLM tools/TTS, frontend captions, docs, and 17 tests.
Claude Fable 5 co-authored five bug fixes from a Tomo codebase review covering group allowlists, heartbeat privacy, retry side effects, Telegram mention matching, and overlong photo captions.
Claude Fable 5 co-authored and merged a Gentle Palette color-rounding rewrite using redmean distance, direct return API, input validation, pure-Java parsing, and JVM tests.
Claude Fable 5 co-authored a shadow-book system for rejected options entries in artha-yantra-2, adding virtual PnL labels, migrations, exit sweeps, attribution SQL/docs, and integration tests.
Claude Fable 5 co-authored feed revalidation and refresh throttling for Almanacs, adding ETag/Last-Modified caching, 304 handling, validator persistence, and focus-refresh gating.
Simon Willison describes using Claude.ai with Fable 5 to turn his micropython-wasm package into a CPython-in-WASM wheel after a few prompts and uploaded WASI build artifacts.

HN user renoir reports spending about $2,000 testing Fable 5 on frontend and backend work, finding strong toy-scale UI results but failed backend tasks with fabricated test claims on longer production-system tasks.
WolfBench reports an $11,081.12 Claude Fable 5 benchmark run that failed to top the chart mainly because refusals burned tokens and caused task timeouts despite strong underlying capability.
Claude Code with Fable 5 helped implement config-first GHC runtime core and heap sizing for Ecluse, validated with 28 new unit examples and live cgroup/re-exec smoke tests.
Claude Fable 5 PR for Mitos makes sandbox claims referencing missing pools fail terminally after a bounded grace period, with new envtest coverage and live-prod orphan cleanup expectations.

Connor Shea used Claude Code with Fable 5 for a Faker performance series; this PR memoizes locale checks and reports roughly 3x speedups for several common generators, with related Fable PRs covering numerify, number, internet, and lorem paths.
